# criblio_cribl_lake_dataset (Resource)

CriblLakeDataset Resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "criblio_cribl_lake_dataset" "my_cribl_lake_dataset" {
  accelerated_fields = [
    "host",
    "status",
  ]
  bucket_name = "lake-tfprovider-beautiful-nguyen-y8y4azd"
  description = "Web server access logs"
  format = "json"
  id = "web_access_logs"
  lake_id = "default"
  retention_period_in_days = 90
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tools/codegen -->
## Schema

### Required

- `id` (String) Unique identifier for the Dataset.
- `lake_id` (String) The <code>id</code> of the Lake to create the Lake Dataset in.

### Optional

- `accelerated_fields` (List of String) Accelerated fields for the Dataset.
- `bucket_name` (String) Name of the legacy Cribl Lake bucket that backs the Dataset.
- `description` (String) Brief description of the Dataset.
- `format` (String)
- `retention_period_in_days` (Integer) Dataset retention period, in days.
- `search_config` (Attributes) Search configuration for the Dataset. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--search_config))
- `storage_class` (String)
- `storage_location_id` (String) Identifier for the Storage Location that backs the Dataset.

<a id="nestedatt--search_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `search_config`

Optional:

- `datatypes` (List of String) Datatype identifiers assigned to the Dataset.
- `metadata` (Attributes) Key-value metadata for the Dataset search configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--search_config--metadata))

<a id="nestedatt--search_config--metadata"></a>
### Nested Schema for `search_config.metadata`

Optional:

- `created` (String) Creation timestamp.
- `enable_acceleration` (Boolean) Whether acceleration is enabled for this dataset.
- `modified` (String) Last modification timestamp.
- `tags` (List of String) Tags associated with the dataset.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = criblio_cribl_lake_dataset.my_criblio_cribl_lake_dataset
  id = jsonencode({
    id      = "web-logs"
    lake_id = "default"
  })
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import criblio_cribl_lake_dataset.my_criblio_cribl_lake_dataset '{"id": "web-logs", "lake_id": "default"}'
```
